channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: How to audit what ChatGPT knows about you - and reclaim your data privacy
item.description: A guide on how to manage personal data privacy settings in ChatGPT is provided, helping users understand what information is collected and how to limit it. This is significant as data privacy concerns grow in AI interactions.

item.title: Hot take: AI's not going to kill open source code security
item.description: Cal.com has shifted from AGPL licensing to a closed commercial codebase, raising concerns among developers about open source security implications. This move reflects broader tensions in the open source community regarding licensing and collaboration.

item.title: Show HN: Harnessing LLM-Prompt Mutation to Build Smart,Automated Fuzz Drivers
item.description: PromptFuzz is introduced as a tool that utilizes LLMs to create automated fuzz drivers for libraries, enhancing software testing efficiency. This innovation could significantly improve the robustness of code by automating a critical testing process.

item.title: Best 2026 MacBook Pro deal: Least expensive M5 Pro now $200 off (Amazon all-time low)
item.description: The 2026 MacBook Pro is currently available at an all-time low price, with discounts of $200 on the M5 Pro model. This deal highlights the competitive pricing strategies in the tech market.

item.title: AI Makes Sitting With Discomfort Feel Shameful
item.description: Ben Nadel discusses the societal pressure to avoid discomfort in the context of AI, suggesting that technology may contribute to feelings of shame. This commentary reflects on the psychological impacts of living in an AI-driven world.

item.title: claude-recall 0.5.4
item.description: The release of Claude Recall 0.5.4 allows for efficient querying of Claude Code session archives using SQLite, enhancing data retrieval capabilities. This update is significant for developers working with Claude Code.

item.title: tokenol 0.2.0
item.description: Tokenol 0.2.0 is launched to audit Claude Code session logs, providing insights into cost tracking and performance metrics. This tool is crucial for developers to optimize their usage of Claude Code.

item.title: claude-gate added to PyPI
item.description: The claude-gate tool is now available on PyPI, allowing users to manage Claude Code access through Slack approvals and execute tasks remotely. This adds a layer of control and convenience for users.

item.title: The Stanford professor behind an FDA-cleared cardiac AI wants $1 billion for his next company
item.description: Stanford professor James Zou is seeking to raise $100 million for his startup Human Intelligence, which focuses on applying AI to human body research, aiming for a valuation of $1 billion. This reflects the growing investment interest in AI healthcare applications.

item.title: iPhone loyalty just hit a new high with only 3.6% of iOS users thinking of switching — but here's why I'm never ditching Android
item.description: iPhone loyalty among users has reached a new high, with only 3.6% considering switching to other platforms, while some users remain committed to Android. This trend indicates strong brand loyalty in the smartphone market.

item.title: cognitx-codegraph 0.1.97
item.description: Cognitx released version 0.1.97 of its Code knowledge graph, enabling indexing and querying of coding languages in Neo4j. This update is significant as it supports Claude Code and AI coding agents.

item.title: mdshrink-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The mdshrink-mcp package was added to PyPI, allowing automatic compression of PDFs and screenshots to Markdown, reducing token usage significantly. This tool is beneficial for optimizing context in Claude Code.

item.title: This InDesign Resume Template Does the First Impression Work for You
item.description: An article discusses a resume template for InDesign, focusing on its layout, typography, and customization. It introduces new editorial frameworks to enhance first impressions.

item.title: Meta signs multibillion-dollar deal for Amazon Graviton5 chips as AI compute demand outstrips $135B capex budget
item.description: Meta has signed a multibillion-dollar deal for Amazon's Graviton5 chips to support AI workloads, reflecting the growing demand for computing power in AI. This investment indicates a strategic shift towards more efficient processing.

item.title: Trump administration targets Chinese firms exploiting US AI models
item.description: The Trump administration is intensifying its crackdown on Chinese firms using US AI models, which could reshape global AI innovation and market dynamics. This move signals a significant escalation in tech rivalry.

item.title: langchain-deepseek-v4 0.1.0
item.description: LangChain has released the deepseek-v4 0.1.0 integration package for DeepSeek chat models, enhancing the capabilities of AI chat applications. This integration is crucial for developers working with these models.

item.title: langchain-deepseek-v4 added to PyPI
item.description: The langchain-deepseek-v4 package was added to PyPI, providing integration for DeepSeek chat models. This addition supports developers in enhancing their AI chat functionalities.

item.title: Meta is firing 8,000 people. Microsoft is paying 8,750 to leave. Both are spending the savings on AI.
item.description: Meta and Microsoft announced significant layoffs affecting 23,000 positions combined, reallocating savings towards AI investments. This reflects a broader trend of tech companies prioritizing AI amidst workforce reductions.

item.title: altergo 0.44.1
item.description: Altergo version 0.44.1 was released, introducing an account session manager for various coding tools. This update aims to streamline user experience across platforms.

item.title: Links 4/25/2026
item.description: A collection of links covering various topics, including global events and economic issues, indicating a diverse range of current affairs. This serves as a snapshot of ongoing discussions.

item.title: From web to Artificial Intelligence: Building the missing links
item.description: The article discusses the evolution of web intelligence and its crucial role in supporting data-driven advancements across various industries. It highlights the increasing complexity of infrastructure needed to manage growing data demands.

item.title: Online event "Smart & Fast. Practical AI Tools for the Modern Java Developer", 06 May
item.description: An online event scheduled for May 6 will focus on practical AI tools for Java developers, emphasizing the shift from basic code suggestions to more advanced Agentic AI capabilities.

item.title: mcp-google-agent-platform-docs 0.1.1
item.description: This article introduces the MCP server that provides documentation for Google's AI platform, specifically the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and Vertex AI, aimed at assisting AI agents.

item.title: Google's Nest Hub has no clue what time it is, and it's messing with our heads
item.description: A user reported that their Google Nest Hub is inaccurately telling the time, leading to confusion and frustration among users.

item.title: torchax 0.0.12.dev20260425
item.description: The article introduces torchax, a new library designed to enable the simultaneous use of Jax and PyTorch, potentially enhancing machine learning workflows.

item.title: I replaced over $1,000 in cloud subscriptions with one small home server
item.description: A user shares their experience of replacing over $1,000 in cloud subscriptions with a single home server, showcasing the potential cost savings of local computing.

item.title: The Pixel 11's new 'Glow' feature is exactly the hardware upgrade I've been waiting for
item.description: The article praises the Pixel 11's new 'Glow' feature as a significant hardware upgrade, marking a notable innovation from Google.

item.title: Deepseek V4 : Why Its 1.6 Trillion Parameters Aren’t Quite Enough
item.description: Deepseek v4 has been tested extensively, revealing its strengths and weaknesses as an open-source AI model, available in two versions for different use cases.

item.title: Elon Musk Fans Increasingly Disgusted by His Toxic Outbursts
item.description: The article discusses growing discontent among Elon Musk's fans regarding his controversial remarks, indicating a shift in public perception.

item.title: p1-hybrid-jailbreak-detector added to PyPI
item.description: A new hybrid jailbreak detector has been added to PyPI, utilizing advanced techniques like ModernBERT and similarity search to enhance security against prompt injection attacks.

item.title: p1-hybrid-jailbreak-detector 0.1.0
item.description: A new hybrid jailbreak and prompt injection detector has been released, utilizing ModernBERT, LoRA, and FAISS for improved performance. This tool aims to enhance security in language models.

item.title: eml-cost-torch 0.1.0a0
item.description: The eml-cost-torch plugin has been introduced, allowing users to analyze Pfaffian depth and width in PyTorch layers. This tool aids in optimizing neural network performance.

item.title: eml-cost-torch added to PyPI
item.description: The eml-cost-torch plugin has been added to PyPI, making it accessible for developers to profile their PyTorch models. This addition supports better model optimization.

item.title: langchain-tool-recover 0.1.0
item.description: LangChain has released a middleware tool for improved tool-call recovery and structured failure messages. This aims to enhance the reliability of AI applications.

item.title: langchain-tool-recover added to PyPI
item.description: The LangChain middleware for tool recovery has been added to PyPI, allowing developers to implement it easily in their projects. This tool enhances error handling in AI workflows.

item.title: nvidia-nat-llama-index 1.7.0a20260425
item.description: NVIDIA has released a subpackage for Llama-Index integration within the NeMo Agent Toolkit, enhancing its capabilities for AI model training and deployment.

item.title: nvidia-nat-langchain 1.7.0a20260425
item.description: A new subpackage for LangChain integration in the NeMo Agent Toolkit has been released by NVIDIA, facilitating better orchestration of AI models.

item.title: destripe 0.1.2
item.description: Destripe 0.1.2 has been introduced, offering a PDHG-based method for removing stripe noise from images using PyTorch. This tool enhances image processing quality.

item.title: protokol-core added to PyPI
item.description: Protokol-core, an ultra-lightweight LLM orchestration framework, has been added to PyPI, providing a new tool for managing language models effectively.

item.title: I'm a Gen Z Microsoft engineer. AI doesn't always save me time, but it's still made my job easier.
item.description: A Gen Z Microsoft engineer shares insights on how AI has made her job easier despite not always saving time. She discusses her experiences and offers career advice.

item.title: subconscious-chat 0.1.3
item.description: Subconscious-chat 0.1.3 is a distributed AI platform that enables the operation of AI agents on various devices. This development enhances accessibility and flexibility in deploying AI solutions.

item.title: subconscious-chat added to PyPI
item.description: Subconscious-chat has been added to PyPI, making it easier for developers to access and implement this distributed AI platform. This move is significant for broadening its user base.

item.title: Show HN: LLMs consume 5.4x less mobile energy than ad-supported web search
item.description: A new analysis shows that large language models (LLMs) consume 5.4 times less energy on mobile devices compared to ad-supported web searches. This insight shifts the energy debate in AI towards mobile efficiency.

item.title: Google Plans to Invest Up to $40 Billion in Anthropic in Two-Phase Deal Tied to Performance Targets
item.description: Google plans to invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic, the AI company behind the Claude models, in a two-phase deal linked to performance targets. This investment underscores Google's commitment to advancing AI technology.

item.title: Meta Signs Agreement With AWS to Power Agentic AI on AWS Graviton Chips
item.description: Meta has signed an agreement with AWS to utilize Graviton chips for powering agentic AI applications. This partnership aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of AI services.

item.title: Remembering Michele Alboreto: F1 ace, Le Mans winner and a good man
item.description: This article pays tribute to Michele Alboreto, a celebrated Formula 1 driver and Le Mans winner, who tragically passed away in 2001. It reflects on his legacy in motorsport.

item.title: Intel’s Diamond Rapids Xeon Slips to 2027 With 512 Cores and 16-Channel Memory, Coral Rapids Brings Back SMT in 2028
item.description: Intel's next-generation Xeon 'Diamond Rapids' CPUs, featuring up to 512 cores, have been delayed to 2027. Meanwhile, the Coral Rapids will reintroduce simultaneous multithreading in 2028.

item.title: India sees fastest global growth in AI hiring at 59.5%: LinkedIn report
item.description: India is experiencing the fastest global growth in AI hiring at 59.5%, with expansion beyond major cities like Bengaluru to others such as Hyderabad and Vijayawada. This trend reflects the increasing demand for AI expertise.

item.title: mcp-google-agent-platform-docs 0.1.0
item.description: The MCP server now provides documentation for Google's AI platform, including the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and Vertex AI. This resource aims to support developers working with AI agents.

item.title: mcp-google-agent-platform-docs added to PyPI
item.description: The MCP server documentation for Google's AI platform has been added to PyPI, making it more accessible for developers. This addition is crucial for those utilizing Google's AI tools.
